findings.summary: Four-wave split was viable; ~120 columns/wave is a good planning target for mechanical doc= scrub work.
DaemonEvent metadata alias trap was handled correctly.
findings.evidence: orch/db/models.py: event_metadata mapped via mapped_column("metadata", ..., doc=...).

findings.summary: Reports mention mixed schema-sourced + inferred descriptions but do not quantify inferred fraction.
findings.recommendation: Add per-wave sourced-vs-inferred counts in future baseline scrubs.

findings.evidence: No diffs in docs/IW_AI_Core_Database_Schema.md or orch/db/migrations/versions/**.

findings.summary: Ordering appears correct: scrub completion preceded baseline delete and gate flip.

findings.evidence: S04 report includes failing and restored make check-column-docs runs after temporary doc blanking.

findings.summary: Retry churn occurred (S01 run1 fail; S04 blocked attempts before completion).
findings.recommendation: Strengthen step prompts with explicit unblock/escalation guidance for gate-flip phases.

findings.summary: S01-S04 use expected content-only n/a TDD RED evidence phrasing.
Direct quantitative comparison with CR-00081/CR-00085 is limited from current worktree artifacts.
findings.recommendation: Persist compact cross-CR metrics (duration, retries, fix cycles, gate failures) in item metadata for future self-assess steps.
